arxiv.title: The Fault in our Stars: Quality Assessment of Code Generation Benchmarks
arxiv.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are gaining popularity among software engineers. A crucial aspect of developing effective code generation LLMs is to evaluate these models using a robust benchmark. Evaluation benchmarks with quality issues can provide a false sense of performance. arxiv.abstract: Large-scale vision 2D vision language models, such as CLIP can be aligned with a 3D encoder to learn generalizable (open-vocabulary) 3D vision models. arxiv.abstract: Earth structural heterogeneities have a remarkable role in the petroleum economy for both exploration and production projects. arxiv.title: Optimal Kernel Tuning Parameter Prediction using Deep Sequence Models
arxiv.abstract: GPU kernels have come to the forefront of computing due to their utility in varied fields, from high-performance computing to machine learning. arxiv.title: Salient Object-Aware Background Generation using Text-Guided Diffusion Models

arxiv.title: SegFormer3D: an Efficient Transformer for 3D Medical Image Segmentation

arxiv.title: TabSQLify: Enhancing Reasoning Capabilities of LLMs Through Table Decomposition
arxiv.abstract: Table reasoning is a challenging task that requires understanding both natural language questions and structured tabular data. arxiv.title: ANCHOR: LLM-driven Subject Conditioning for Text-to-Image Synthesis
arxiv.abstract: Text-to-image (T2I) models have achieved remarkable progress in high-quality image synthesis, yet most benchmarks rely on simple, self-contained prompts, failing to capture the complexity of real-world captions. arxiv.title: Language Model Cascades: Token-level uncertainty and beyond
arxiv.abstract: Recent advances in language models (LMs) have led to significant improvements in quality on complex NLP tasks, but at the expense of increased inference costs.
